_comment: Weekly candidate pool for future Ageimals rounds. Each candidate is a real animal with a cited source. 'born' must be a strict YYYY-MM-DD date (the site computes live age from it). 'named' animals are prioritized to the top of the active list; unnamed newborns are still fine. 'used' animals have already been built into a round host sheet and should not be suggested again. Keep 3-5 entries with used=false at all times — top up with fresh finds when the active count drops below 3.

candidates.name: Timmy
candidates.hook: Turned 9 at the Louisville Zoo's July Birthday Bash. Amur tigers rarely reach double digits in the wild, which throws guesses off.

candidates.name: Bella
candidates.hook: Turned 42 at the same Birthday Bash — orangutans can live past 50 in human care, so "grown up" is a long way from "old" for her.

candidates.name: Albus
candidates.hook: The tusked "deer-pig" of Sulawesi turned 17. Most guessers have never seen the species before, let alone know its lifespan.

candidates.name: Unnamed gorilla calf
candidates.hook: Only the second gorilla ever born at the Detroit Zoo, to first-time mom Tulivu and dad Mshindi — half-sibling to 2024's Motema.

candidates.name: Unnamed leopard cub
candidates.hook: The first Amur leopard ever born at the zoo in its 144-year history, to mom Marta and dad Sylas.
